display_name: CRC counters are cumulative since last clear — check if incrementing now
context: show interface output displaying CRC, input errors, or other error counters
observation: CRC errors or input errors are non-zero in show interface output
naive_interpretation: The interface has a physical layer problem causing CRC errors right now
correct_interpretation: The counters are cumulative since the last counter clear or device reload. Non-zero CRCs only indicate a current problem if the counters are actively incrementing during the issue window. With weeks of uptime and no counter clears, even a healthy interface may show a small number of CRCs from past transient events.
why: Interface counters on all major NOS platforms are cumulative — they count since boot or last clear counters command
There is no timestamp on individual counter values showing when the errors occurred
A device with 90 days of uptime showing 50 CRCs may have had all 50 occur during a cable swap two months ago

confirmation_commands.purpose: Check error counters — run twice with a 30-60 second gap to see if counters are incrementing
Snapshot the specific counters to compare against a second sample
Reset counters to zero and then monitor for new errors appearing
Reset counters and monitor for new errors

traps: Seeing non-zero CRCs and immediately blaming the cable or optic without checking if the errors are current
Replacing an SFP or cable based on stale counter data that predates the current issue
Not checking system uptime to contextualize counter magnitude — 10 CRCs in 90 days is very different from 10 CRCs in 5 minutes
Forgetting that a reload clears counters but a configuration change does not
